Module com.cuatrifasico.battleshi
Package com.cuatrifasico.battleshi.view.shapes

Class ShipShapeFactory

java.lang.Object
com.cuatrifasico.battleshi.view.shapes.ShipShapeFactory
public final class ShipShapeFactory extends Object
Builds the JavaFX Group representing a ship's visual body.

Every ship is composed as a sequence of individual, cell-sized pieces (one Shape per occupied cell) laid out at exact multiples of BoardTheme.CELL_SIZE, rather than as a single stretched rectangle. This guarantees the ship aligns perfectly with the board grid regardless of length, and lets each end piece be rounded only on its outward-facing corners while staying sharp on the corners that connect to the next segment:

  • Frigate (1 cell): a single square, fully rounded on all four corners using BoardTheme.CELL_ARC (same radius as board cells).
  • Destroyer (2 cells) / Submarine (3 cells): two end caps rounded with BoardTheme.SHIP_CAPSULE_ARC (a full semicircle), plus a plain square body between them for the submarine.
  • Aircraft carrier (4 cells): two end caps rounded with the smaller BoardTheme.CELL_ARC, plus two plain square body segments between them.
Because ship sprites are symmetric along their own axis, rotating a ship never requires an actual 90-degree transform: the caller only needs to swap which axis (row vs. column) segments are laid out along, which is exactly what the orientation parameter controls here.

    Method
    Description
    private static javafx.scene.shape.Shape
    createEndCap(double arc, boolean outwardIsLeadingSide, Orientation orientation)
    Builds a single cell-sized end cap: a fully rounded square unioned with a plain half-rectangle covering the inward half, which masks the rounding away on the side facing the rest of the ship and leaves it only on the outward-facing corners.

  • Method Details

    • createShipNode

      public static javafx.scene.Group createShipNode(ShipType shipType, Orientation orientation, String fillStyleClass)
      Creates the visual body of a ship, including the darker hit-point dots marking where each segment will show a hit marker later. Each piece is tagged with fillStyleClass so its color comes entirely from the stylesheet (e.g. the normal navy body, or a dark red "sunk & revealed" variant).
      Parameters:
      shipType - The ship category, defines size and corner style.
      orientation - The orientation to render the ship in.
      fillStyleClass - The CSS class controlling this ship's fill color.
      Returns:
      A Group sized to exactly cover the ship's footprint (size * CELL_SIZE along its length), top-left at local (0,0).

    • createShipNode

      public static javafx.scene.Group createShipNode(ShipType shipType, Orientation orientation)
      Convenience overload using the default "ship-body" style class.
      Parameters:
      shipType - The ship category.
      orientation - The orientation to render the ship in.
      Returns:
      The ship's visual Group.

    • createSegmentPiece

      private static javafx.scene.shape.Shape createSegmentPiece(int size, int segment, Orientation orientation)

    • createUniformRoundedSquare

      private static javafx.scene.shape.Rectangle createUniformRoundedSquare(double arc)

    • createEndCap

      private static javafx.scene.shape.Shape createEndCap(double arc, boolean outwardIsLeadingSide, Orientation orientation)
      Builds a single cell-sized end cap: a fully rounded square unioned with a plain half-rectangle covering the inward half, which masks the rounding away on the side facing the rest of the ship and leaves it only on the outward-facing corners.
      Parameters:
      arc - The corner radius to use for the rounded half.
      outwardIsLeadingSide - true if the outward side is the left (horizontal) / top (vertical) side.
      orientation - The ship's orientation.
      Returns:
      The composed Shape for this end cap.

    • positionSegment

      private static void positionSegment(javafx.scene.shape.Shape piece, int segment, Orientation orientation)

    • createHitDot

      private static javafx.scene.shape.Circle createHitDot(int segment, Orientation orientation)

    • createPointedEndCap

      private static javafx.scene.shape.Shape createPointedEndCap(boolean outwardIsLeadingSide, Orientation orientation)
